items.title: GIFT Nifty up 90 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: Indian equity markets closed Tuesday on a cautious, range-bound note, with profit booking in banking, IT, and healthcare stocks acting as a drag. Analysts anticipate a mildly positive near-term outlook with a consolidative bias, expecting upside to be capped unless a clear breakout above 26,000 occurs.

items.title: Stocks in news: M&M, LG, Lenskart, BHEL, Titan, Shoppers Stop
items.description: Markets ended marginally higher on weekly expiry day, with analysts maintaining a bullish stance and recommending a buy-on-dips strategy. Several companies like M&M, LG, and Titan are in focus due to Q3 results and other corporate developments, including BHEL's government stake sale and RIL's acquisition.

items.title: Surplus liquidity fuels record parking in SDF
items.description: Banks have parked a record ₹4.52 lakh crore in the RBI's Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) at 5% overnight. This strategy allows them to borrow funds at lower rates, around 4.27% from the TREPS market and earn a significant spread by depositing with the central bank. This reflects substantial surplus liquidity in the banking system.

items.title: Govt launches OFS to divest up to 5% in BHEL
items.description: The government is offloading up to 5% of its stake in BHEL through an Offer for Sale, starting with 3% and an option for an additional 2%. Bids open Wednesday for institutional investors and Thursday for retail, with a floor price set at ₹254 per share. This move aims to reduce government holding and boost public ownership and stock liquidity.

items.title: CRAs need to maintain additional net worth: Sebi
items.description: Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) mandates credit rating agencies (CRAs) to maintain additional net worth for rating instruments under other financial sector regulators. This move follows industry representations to allow rating of unlisted securities and other financial products, potentially creating synergies and addressing market gaps.

items.title: After Tiger Global ruling, Income Tax dept sends notices to foreign VCs, PE funds
items.description: India's tax department is intensifying scrutiny of foreign investment firms. Following a Supreme Court decision, notices have been issued to several overseas venture capital and private equity houses. The tax office is seeking detailed information about their operations in Mauritius and Singapore.

items.title: Piramal Finance raises $400 million in ECB from Global, local lenders including Deutsche Bank AG & SMBC
items.description: Piramal Finance secured $400 million through an external commercial borrowing (ECB) package from a consortium including Deutsche Bank, SMBC, Axis Bank, DBS, and Far Eastern International Bank. The 42-month facility will see about half the principal repaid as a bullet at maturity, priced at SOFR +175 bps.

items.title: Maharashtra govt told to pay ₹691 cr to IDRCL in tollway case
items.description: India Debt Resolution Co (IDRCL), the managing arm of NARCL, secured a ₹691 crore arbitration award from Bombay High Court-appointed arbitrator Justice JP Devadhar. This is nearly six times the ₹117 crore IDRCL paid to acquire Sion Panvel Tollways’ (SPTPL) debt in 2024, marking one of the first recoveries for the government-backed bad bank since 2022.

items.title: Banks ease pace of hiring as tech, AI shoulder more tasks
items.description: Indian banks are slowing hiring due to automation, AI, and digital tools reducing the need for large staff increases. HDFC Bank added 5,000 employees in December 2025, reaching 2,15,000, while Axis Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank saw declines in headcount.

items.title: Reliance Industries, SBI among 10 Nifty50 stocks where FII increased holdings in Q3. Check full list
items.description: Even as FIIs continued to pare exposure, offloading more than Rs 1.6 lakh crore worth of equities last year, the latest Q3 data shows foreign ownership increased in 10 stocks, indicating selective buying despite an overall cautious stance. Here’s what you need to know.

items.title: Jio Financial, HDFC Bank among 10 Nifty50 stocks that saw decrease in FII holding in Q3
items.description: While domestic institutional investors are playing an increasingly dominant role in India’s benchmark indices, FIIs continue to pare exposure, having sold more than Rs 1.6 lakh crore worth of equities last year. Data shows FII ownership declined by 90 basis points YoY and 20 basis points QoQ to 24.3% in Q3, with holdings reduced across 39 stocks. Here are 10 major names that saw a decline in FII exposure.

items.title: Sensex rises over 100 pts, Nifty reclaims 26,000 as auto, IT stocks rally
items.description: Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty opened higher on Wednesday, extending their gains for a fourth consecutive session. Strong buying in auto, IT, metal, and FMCG stocks fueled the early trade, with the Nifty 50 reclaiming the 26,000 mark. However, the broader market experienced profit booking.

items.title: Three profitable quarters later, is Paytm's comeback strong enough to outlast multibagger returns?
items.description: Paytm has achieved its third profitable quarter, demonstrating a sustained recovery. Investors are now focused on the company's long-term durability and the quality of its growth. While core businesses like payments and financial services are performing well, new growth drivers like artificial intelligence are being explored.

items.title: Fallen angels return: Smallcaps stocks emerge from the shadows as Q3 earnings finally flip the script
items.description: Smallcap stocks are showing signs of recovery after a challenging 2025, outperforming larger peers in Q3 earnings. Analysts see this as a potential turning point, with earnings upgrades expected in 2026, though a gradual recovery and selective stock picking remain crucial for investors.

items.title: Swiggy among 4 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.description: On February 10, four stocks in the Nifty 200 segment that gained over 3% were highlighted in the RSI Trending Up scan, based on data from StockEdge.com. It is considered an uptrend signal if the RSI value has crossed above 50 from below. “RSI trending up” means that the Relative Strength Index (RSI) values are increasing, suggesting strengthening momentum in the price of the stock. This can indicate potential upward movement and may be used by traders to identify buying opportunities.

items.title: Eicher Motors shares jump over 5% on strong Q3 numbers. Should you buy, sell or hold?
items.description: Eicher Motors reported a strong December quarter, led by robust profit growth, solid revenue expansion and healthy operational performance. Royal Enfield’s capacity expansion plans and improved performance at VE Commercial Vehicles supported optimism. The stock remains technically strong, trading above key averages with neutral momentum.

items.title: Apollo Hospitals shares up 4% after Q3 net profit jumps 35%. Should you buy, sell or hold?
items.description: Apollo Hospitals announced impressive third-quarter earnings. Net profit surged 35% year-on-year to Rs 502 crore. Revenue grew 17% to Rs 6,477 crore. All business segments showed strong growth. The company continues to invest in advanced clinical capabilities. Citi maintains a Buy rating with a target price of Rs 9,600.

items.title: MSCI Rejig: Aditya Birla Capital, L&T Finance in, IRCTC out. Here’s everything you need to know
items.description: MSCI's February 2026 index review sees Aditya Birla Capital and L&T Finance added to the Global Standard Index, while IRCTC is removed. India's overall weight remains steady, but the number of companies in the Standard Index rises. The Small Cap Index, however, experiences significant removals.

items.title: Titan Company shares tick 3% higher as Q3 profit jumps 61% YoY
items.description: Titan Company shares are in focus after a strong Q3FY26 performance. Robust festive demand fueled growth across segments. Net profit rose 61% year-on-year. The jewellery segment was the main driver, with revenues up 42%. Watches and Wearables also saw strong festive traction. EyeCare delivered steady growth. The company remains focused on brand equity and sustainable growth.

items.title: Positive Breakout: These 11 stocks close cross above their 200 DMA
items.description: In the Nifty500 pack, 15 stocks' closing prices crossed above their 200 DMA (Daily Moving Averages) on February 10, 2026. Of these, we have highlighted 11 stocks that posted gains of more than 2%, according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. The 200-day daily moving average (DMA) is used by traders as a key indicator to determine the overall trend in a particular stock. As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily timeframe, it is generally considered to be in an overall uptrend. Take a look:

items.title: Vodafone Idea rallies 76% in 6 months; brokerages turn cautious
items.description: Vodafone Idea shares are experiencing a significant rally. This surge is fueled by government relief on AGR dues, a new three-year turnaround plan, and increased investment from promoter Kumar Mangalam Birla. While operating losses are narrowing and ARPU is improving, experts advise caution due to ongoing challenges with subscriber growth and ARPU increases.

items.title: Samvardhana Motherson shares soar over 5% after Q3. Here’s what Nomura, Citi, Motilal said
items.description: Samvardhana Motherson International's stock climbed 5% following a strong third quarter profit rise. Analysts from Motilal Oswal and Nomura recommend buying the stock, citing growth potential and acquisitions. Citi, however, maintains a sell rating due to global demand risks. The company aims for significant revenue growth in the coming years.

items.title: BHEL shares fall 6% after Rs 4,422 crore OFS opens for subscription
items.description: BHEL shares dropped nearly 5% to Rs 259.3 as the government launched an Offer for Sale (OFS) to divest up to 5% stake, aiming to raise approximately Rs 4,422 crore. The floor price is set at Rs 254 per share, a discount to Tuesday's closing. The offer opened for non-retail investors on Wednesday and for retail investors on Thursday.

items.title: Swaps shut the door on India rate cuts, growth-inflation outlook lifts longer tenors
items.description: India's overnight indexed swaps signal that the central bank's ​rate-cut cycle has likely run ​its course, anchoring short-term rates, while longer-tenor rates are being pulled ​higher on expectations of firmer inflation and robust growth.

items.title: HUL Q3 Preview: Will GST transition dent profit, revenue growth for consumer goods major?
items.description: Hindustan Unilever Ltd is set to reveal its third-quarter financial results. Revenue growth is anticipated to be modest. Adjusted profit after tax may see a slight decrease. This is due to changes in the Goods and Services Tax regime. Demand stabilization is showing early signs. Investors will watch for management insights on rural recovery and pricing power.

items.title: Eicher Motors' target price raised up to Rs 8,800. What Jefferies, CLSA, other brokerages say
items.description: Eicher Motors shares jumped after the company reported strong Q3 results, with net profit rising 21% year-on-year to Rs 1,420 crore and revenue growing 23%. Brokerages also raised price targets, seeing potential upside in the stock.

items.title: Promoters continuously selling stakes at high valuations, Nifty 500 ownership slips to historic low: Motilal Oswal
items.description: Promoters are reducing stakes in Indian listed companies to an all-time low of nearly 49% in December 2025, driven by high valuations and strong investor appetite. Meanwhile, domestic institutional investors have increased their ownership to a record 20.6%, while foreign institutional investors and retail investors saw a dip.

items.title: Samvardhana Motherson shares soar 5% after Q3. Here’s what Nomura, Citi, Motilal said
items.description: Samvardhana Motherson International's stock climbed following a strong third quarter profit rise. Analysts are optimistic, reiterating buy ratings and increasing price targets. The company's growth is fueled by acquisitions and organic expansion. Future prospects look bright with ambitious revenue goals and market positioning.

items.title: MSCI Rejig: 7 smallcap stocks included in latest shuffle. Do you own any?
items.description: MSCI has unveiled the outcome of its February 2026 index review, announcing changes to the MSCI Global Standard Indexes. As part of the latest reshuffle, seven new smallcap stocks across multiple sectors have been added. The revisions are expected to trigger passive fund flows and may influence near-term stock movements as global index-tracking funds realign their portfolios.

items.title: BHEL OFS: Why JM Financial suggests investors buy the stock while projecting a 40% upside?
items.description: The government's BHEL OFS is priced at Rs 254, offering a valuation of 21x FY28 earnings. Analysts see significant upside, driven by India's coal capacity roadmap and BHEL's strong order book. Margin expansion and growth in nuclear and coal gasification further bolster the bullish outlook.

items.title: Textile stocks fall up to 6% for second day. How serious is the Bangladesh threat?
items.description: Shares of major Indian textile companies fell up to 6% for a second straight day after Bangladesh signed a trade agreement with the U.S., offering lower tariffs and exemptions on select textiles. Experts suggest the impact on Indian exporters may be limited, though competitive pressures could rise, particularly in product-specific categories.

items.title: Market Wrap: Sensex snaps 3-day gain, Nifty holds 25,900 as IT selloff dampens sentiment
items.description: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended largely flat on Wednesday, with the 30-share Sensex snapping its three-day gaining streak, while Nifty managed to close marginally higher.

items.title: SBI overtakes TCS to become India's fourth largest company as Q3 rally triggers market cap leap
items.description: State Bank of India has surpassed TCS to become the fourth-largest listed firm by market capitalisation after a sharp rally driven by strong quarterly earnings. Robust profit growth, stable margins, improving asset quality and bullish brokerage views boosted investor sentiment.

items.title: BHEL triggers oversubscription option after OFS sees 2.35 times demand. Check retail quota
items.description: State-run Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL) on Wednesday informed the exchanges about its intent to exercise the oversubscription option after the offer for sale received strong response from non-retail investors.

items.title: Two Trades for Today: A pharmaceutical major for a 6.7% rise, a mid-cap banking stock for a 6% gain
items.description: Our technical analysts identify select stocks that may gain momentum even in volatile markets. Here are the technical calls for today.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: Eicher Motors and BHEL among 6 stocks that moved most on Wednesday
Vodafone Idea attracts steady MF buying, promoter ups stake. Time to buy, sell or hold?
items.description: Vodafone Idea is witnessing steady accumulation by domestic mutual funds and promoter Kumar Mangalam Birla, even as it reports continued losses. Improving revenue, margins and operational metrics signal gradual stabilisation, though profitability and valuations remain key concerns for investors.

items.title: BSE Index Services launches 2 new G-Sec indices
items.description: BSE Index Services, an arm of the BSE, on Wednesday announced the launch of two new G-Sec indices, which can be used for running passive strategies such as ETFs (exchange traded funds) and Index Funds.

items.title: Valuation Watch: 10 Midcaps with sky-high P/E ratios
items.description: A price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio above 75 suggests that a company’s stock price is rising significantly faster than its earnings. The metric is calculated using the latest end-of-day price and trailing twelve months (TTM) earnings per share. Based on a valuation scan by Stockedge.com, we have identified the top 10 NSE midcap stocks with the highest TTM P/E ratios as of the December ’25 quarter.While a high P/E multiple reflects strong growth expectations, it may also indicate potential overvaluation or speculative buying. Investors should therefore assess the company’s fundamentals, earnings visibility, and broader market conditions before concluding.

items.title: 10 Nifty500 stocks crash 50–75% from 52-week highs. Is your portfolio hit?
items.description: The benchmark Nifty50 and the broader Nifty500 are hovering just 1.5% below their 52-week highs—barely a dent on the surface. To a casual observer, it may appear to be business as usual on Dalal Street. A closer look, however, tells a very different story.Beneath the indices’ steady facade, several individual stocks have undergone steep corrections. Within the Nifty500 universe, nearly 160 companies have fallen between 25% and 75% from their respective 52-week peaks. A deeper dive into this battered segment reveals 10 stocks that have plunged more than 50% from their yearly highs—effectively halving investor wealth even as the broader index shows little visible stress.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.description: Indian benchmark indices ended mixed in a range-bound session, with Nifty extending gains for a fourth day while Sensex edged lower. Analysts see bullish momentum above key support levels, eyeing a breakout past 26,000. Global cues were mixed, with US optimism and subdued European trends.

items.title: Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs pick multibagger Ather Energy via block deals; NIIF sells Rs 233 cr stake
items.description: Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs acquired shares in Ather Energy through block deals worth Rs 233 crore, as NIIF sold over 33.78 lakh shares. Several mutual funds also bought stakes. The smallcap multibagger stock remains up 123% from its IPO despite recent declines.

items.title: MRPL among 4 smallcap stocks that hit 52-week highs & rallied up to 40% in a month
items.description: On Wednesday, the benchmark Sensex slipped slightly by around 40 points, closing at 84,233. Despite an otherwise flat market, four BSE 250 small-cap stocks hit their 52-week highs, reaching their strongest levels in the past year. Market participants see these breakouts as bullish indicators, signaling strong upward momentum and the potential for further gains.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Craftsman Automation and Laurus Labs offer up to 12% upside
items.description: Nifty ended flat but extended gains, with technical indicators signaling a bullish bias above key support. Analysts highlight Craftsman Automation and Laurus Labs as buy ideas, citing strong uptrend structure, base formation, improving momentum and potential upside on breakout.

items.title: Sebi asks commodity exchanges to maintain two times of projected peak load capacity
items.description: Sebi on Wednesday came out with a new infrastructure planning and real-time performance monitoring framework for exchanges and clearing corporations operating in the commodity derivatives segment, mandating that critical IT systems maintain installed capacity of at least two times the projected peak load.

items.title: Patanjali Foods Q3 Results: PAT surges 60% YoY to Rs 594 crore, sees strong FY26 on GST tailwinds
items.description: Patanjali Foods witnessed a robust 60% surge in net profit to Rs 594 crore for the December quarter, with revenue climbing 17% to Rs 10,484 crore. This marks their highest Q3 revenue ever. Both FMCG and edible oil segments showed significant year-on-year growth, indicating strong market performance and positive future outlook.

items.title: These mid-cap stocks with ‘Strong Buy’ & ‘Buy’ recos can rally over 25%, according to analysts
items.description: As an investor, it is good to look ahead to how the earnings and fundamentals of a sector are panning out, even while maintaining a bullish stance. The street is clearly indicating that, in the near-term at least, it is going to be selective even if it has a bullish bias. Only if earnings have been better in Q3 (and Q4 earnings visibility is good) will it likely reward a stock.

items.title: Indus Tower stock hits fresh record highs in February; what should traders do?
items.description: Indus Towers stock has surged to new record highs after breaking a month-long consolidation. Experts recommend buying the stock for a potential target of Rs 480 within the next three to four weeks. The stock has shown strong upward momentum, supported by technical indicators. Traders can consider buying around Rs 455 with a stop loss below Rs 436.
